title: Upnishad Arya Bhashya, Dutiya Bhag
gloss: A translation of the Chhendogya and Brihadaranyak Upanishadas into Hindi, with an independent commentary in the light of the Arya Samaj tenets by Pandit Muni Ram, Professor, Dayanand-Anglo-Vedic College, Lahore.
copyright: Pandit Dev Datt Sharma, Lahore; No. 156, dated the 25th October, 1910.
notes: 1,108-page Chandogya + Brihadaranyaka with Arya Samaj commentary by a DAV College professor, copyrighted — institutional Vedanta at monument scale; bracket note: previous part noticed in entry No. 9 at page 25 of the Catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th September, 1909.

flags.issue: margin serial partially cut; read as 21 (follows 20 on p34)

author: Imam Bakhsh, Mian
title: Kitab Fawaid-ul-Mubtidi
gloss: A booklet in which Sindhi sentences are given with their equivalents in Persian for the use of beginners.
notes: Section carries '(Designed for educational purposes.)'. Pokhar Das of Shikarpur again (cf. p17) — his Sindhi list now includes a Persian phrase-book; reg 1231 sits in his 1232–1233 batch.

author: Muhammad Masum, Maulvi
title: Risalat-ush Shauq
gloss: Verses in the praise of the author's spiritual teacher.
notes: Pir-praise verse registered from Quetta — Baluchistan's second appearance this quarter. Dual date: 1328 Hijri / 17th September 1910.

title: Prashan Upanishad
gloss: The Prashn Upnishad, with a literal translation by Swami Darshna Nand.
notes: Upanishad with Urdu translation — Sanskrit scripture crossing into the Persian-script reading public via the Arya press.

flags.issue: margin serial obscured by pencil X; read as 3

section: BILINGUAL (SANSKRIT IN PERSIAN CHARACTER AND URDU)—LANGUAGE.
author: Dwarka Prasad, Munshi
title: Bishan Sahansar Nam
gloss: A glossary of the difficult Sanskrit words occurring in the celebrated religious poem, Vishnu Sahasr Nam, with their meanings in Urdu.
notes: The registry invents a category for it: SANSKRIT IN PERSIAN CHARACTER — the Vishnu Sahasranama read by devotees literate only in Urdu script; 2nd edition implies real demand. The catalog's taxonomy stretching to fit script-crossed piety.

section: BILINGUAL (PANJABI IN PERSIAN CHARACTER AND URDU)—POETRY.
author: Habibulla 'Asi, Munshi
title: Si Harfi
publisher: B. Chatar Singh Jiwan Singh, Book-sellers
notes: Muslim poet, Sikh booksellers (Chatar Singh Jiwan Singh), si-harfi acrostic form — reg 1329 completes the Silcock cluster 1328–1332.
